title: remove hack/dind
body:
The docker-in-docker was script was needed to run AppArmor tests in
Travis, which is not required anymore since Travis isn't being used
for a while.  Removing the script will also cure some hiccups on
some atomic testing nodes.

Should we have a pinp script?
@vrothberg What was broken before?
@rhatdan we dont know what is going on

@rhatdan, we are getting closer. The dind script was needed to run the tests in the Docker containers in Travis (Ubuntu) but the env has changed now. It's still a bit confusing that it started breaking now.

My brain might be tired but I am out of ideas. The dind is now entirely removed and we're getting things like:
```
.papr.sh: line 90: /go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/hack/selinux_tag.sh: Permission denied
```
Permission denied, always makes me think SELinux.  Is there a way to see if SELinux is causing issues on the nodes?  AVC Messages?  RUn tests in permissive mode.
I think we need to debug on one of those nodes. It feels a bit of a whack a mole and I certainly spent my energy budget :D
